description: Trigger discrimination: SKILL retirement planning (this skill) vs DOC lifecycle/retirement (docs-retention-index — now shipped; the seam stays pinned so neither absorbs the other), usage-evidence design (skill-usage-instrumenter), the removal PR's review (library-diff-reviewer), and the quality verdict that may precede retirement (skill-quality-reviewer).

cases.prompt: Skill X is superseded by skill Y — plan X's safe exit: mark it, redirect its triggers, and remove it without breaking the neighbors whose yield clauses and trigger-evals name it.
cases.reason: A named skill with an established supersession trigger needs the staged lifecycle plan — reverse-link dispositions, mark/redirect/remove, rollback. No PR exists yet to review, no evidence needs gathering, no quality verdict is being asked for.

cases.prompt: Build the index that governs our workflow docs' lifecycle — retention category per doc, reason-to-keep, superseded-by chains, and cleanup rules for the stale ones.
cases.note: SEAM CASE — docs-retention-index is now shipped and owns doc lifecycle end-to-end; skill-deprecation-planner must NOT absorb doc lifecycle, and this planner's subject stays the executable skill directories under the library's skills path. The two discriminate on artifact type — SKILL vs DOC.
cases.reason: Retention categories, superseded-by chains, and cleanup rules for DOCUMENTS are doc-lifecycle governance. A skill is an invocable procedure with triggers, evals, and registration arithmetic; a doc is prose with a retention category — different artifact, different lifecycle owner.

cases.prompt: Before we retire anything, how would we even find out whether these older skills still get invoked by anyone?
cases.reason: The question is how usage evidence ACCRUES — signals, tiers, windows. The planner starts once evidence (or another qualifying trigger) exists; designing the measurement is the instrumenter's whole job, and 'before we retire anything' does not start a retirement.

cases.prompt: Here's the PR that deletes the deprecated skill and updates the catalog, README, and the neighbors' trigger-evals — review it before merge.
cases.reason: An opened retirement PR needs the executed diff checked — rows moved not vanished, no dangling references, counts decremented, plan conformance. That is the PR-level review; the planner authored the plan the reviewer now checks against.

cases.prompt: Is this shipped skill even worth keeping — does it duplicate the newer one we added last month, or does it still earn its place?
cases.reason: Whether a skill duplicates a neighbor is a quality/duplication verdict (check 3 — possibly make-it-an-extension). The planner consumes such a verdict as its absorbed trigger; it does not form the judgment. Verdict first, retirement plan second.
